```{=html}

Data with a Plot

Lindsey E. Wylie, J.D., Ph.D.

Applied Research Program Evaluation Measurement & Design Data Storytelling
See my work About me
``` --- ::: {.project-grid} ::: {.project-card} Data Storytelling

The Hip-Hop Periodic Table

139 artists. Five lyricism dimensions. A fully documented dataset built to teach measurement validity, sampling bias, uncertainty communication, and visualization design — using a subject matter executives actually argue about.

R / Quarto Interactive Teaching module

Explore the dashboard → ::: ::: --- ## About {#about .section-heading} ::: {.bio-block}
LEW
::: {} I work at the intersection of data, law, and organizational decision-making. My non-work work focuses on making rigorous analytical thinking accessible — through unexpected datasets, honest methodology, and visualizations that show their own uncertainty. ::: {.credential-badges} PhD — [Social Psychology, certificate in Research Methods and Data Analysis] JD — [specialized in Health Law] [Senior Research Associate / National Center for State Courts] ::: I'm particularly interested in the gap between how organizations say they make decisions and how they actually make decisions — and in the role that poorly designed measurement plays in that gap. When I'm not doing that, I'm building models of hip-hop artists. ::: ::: --- ## What I do {.section-heading} ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="33%"} **Teaching** Executive and graduate education in data storytelling, measurement design, and evidence-based decision-making. I use real datasets with documented flaws — because clean pedagogical data teaches the wrong lessons. ::: ::: {.column width="33%"} **Research** Applied work at the boundary of law, psychology, and data storytelling. Particular interest in construct validity, uncertainty quantification, and the politics of how metrics get built and used. ::: ::: {.column width="33%"} **Projects** Independent projects that translate methodological ideas into interactive, shareable artifacts. The hip-hop table is one. More in [the projects gallery](projects/index.html). ::: ::: --- ::: {style="text-align: center; padding: 3rem 0 1rem; color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.9rem;"} Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) · Hosted on [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com) :::
